groups.context_description: Term names divine giving as the expression of covenant faithfulness — God's giving of land, promise, and blessing as the embodiment of his inner covenantal commitment

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 12:7 Then the Lord appeared to Abram and said , “ To your offspring I will give this land .” So he built there an altar to the Lord , who had appeared to him .
Gen 15:18 On that day the Lord made a covenant with Abram , saying , “ To your offspring I give this land , from the river of Egypt to the great river , the river Euphrates ,

groups.context_description: Term names divine giving as the expression of inner love and compassion — God gives in direct response to inner need, longing, prayer, or suffering

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 29:33 She conceived again and bore a son , and said , “ Because the Lord has heard that I am hated , he has given me this son also .” And she called his name Simeon .

Hos 11:8 How can I give you up, O Ephraim ? How can I hand you over, O Israel ? How can I make you like Admah ? How can I treat you like Zeboiim ? My heart recoils within me; my compassion grows warm and tender .

groups.context_description: Term names the giving of inner-being capacities — wisdom, spirit, understanding, songs, and strength given directly to the inner life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Neh 9:20 You gave your good Spirit to instruct them and did not withhold your manna from their mouth and gave them water for their thirst .
Job 38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts or given understanding to the mind ?

groups.context_description: Term names divine giving as moral judgement — God gives into the hand of enemies or destruction as the moral expression of his inner response to faithlessness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jer 12:7 “I have forsaken my house ; I have abandoned my heritage ; I have given the beloved of my soul into the hands of her enemies .
Hos 13:11 I gave you a king in my anger , and I took him away in my wrath .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner act of human giving — self-giving, sacrificial giving, votive giving, or giving as the expression of inner orientation toward God or community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 1:21 And he said , “ Naked I came from my mother’s womb , and naked shall I return . The Lord gave , and the Lord has taken away ; blessed be the name of the Lord .”
Isa 50:6 I gave my back to those who strike , and my cheeks to those who pull out the beard ; I hid not my face from disgrace and spitting .

groups.context_description: Term names the giving or withholding of glory and honour — the inner-being act of ascribing worth to God, or divine withholding of glory from others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 42:8 I am the Lord ; that is my name ; my glory I give to no other , nor my praise to carved idols .

Jer 13:16 Give glory to the Lord your God before he brings darkness , before your feet stumble on the twilight mountains , and while you look for light he turns it into gloom and makes it deep darkness .

groups.context_description: Term names eschatological or restorative giving — God gives to transform inner conditions: ashes to beauty, mourning to gladness, faint spirit to praise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 61:3 to grant to those who mourn in Zion — to give them a beautiful headdress instead of ashes , the oil of gladness instead of mourning , the garment of praise instead of a faint spirit ; that they may be called oaks of righteousness , the planting of the Lord , that he may be glorified .

Hos 2:15 And there I will give her her vineyards and make the Valley of Achor a door of hope . And there she shall answer as in the days of her youth , as at the time when she came out of the land of Egypt .

groups.context_description: Term declares God's inner being as good — the foundational doxological assertion that God's character, name, steadfast love, and Spirit are good, and that his goodness governs his relationship to all he has made

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 34:8 Oh, taste and see that the Lord is good ! Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him !
Psa 119:68 You are good and do good ; teach me your statutes .

groups.context_description: Term names human moral character and conduct as good — the inner quality of the person who walks uprightly, acts honestly, and whose character is recognised and assessed as good before God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mic 6:8 He has told you , O man , what is good ; and what does the Lord require of you but to do justice , and to love kindness , and to walk humbly with your God ?

groups.context_description: Term names goodness as inner experiential good — what is genuinely good for the human person: proximity to God, worship, waiting, communal harmony, and the reorientation of the inner life toward what truly satisfies

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 73:28 But for me it is good to be near God ; I have made the Lord God my refuge , that I may tell of all your works .

groups.context_description: Term names comparative wisdom good — the better-than sayings of wisdom literature where inner and relational qualities are ranked as the greater good above material wealth, social status, or external abundance

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 15:16 Better is a little with the fear of the Lord than great treasure and trouble with it .
Pro 16:32 Whoever is slow to anger is better than the mighty , and he who rules his spirit than he who takes a city .

groups.context_description: Term names God's good word and promise — the covenantal faithfulness through which God declares, fulfils, and sustains his promised good toward his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jos 23:14 “And now I am about to go the way of all the earth , and you know in your hearts and souls , all of you, that not one word has failed of all the good things that the Lord your God promised concerning you. All have come to pass for you; not one of them has failed .

groups.context_description: Term names the moral assessment of conduct, ways, or deeds as not good — the prophetic and wisdom verdict that certain inner orientations and behavioural expressions are contrary to the good God requires

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 36:31 Then you will remember your evil ways , and your deeds that were not good , and you will loathe yourselves for your iniquities and your abominations .

groups.context_description: Term names the state of inner wellbeing, pleasure, or moral approval — the heart merry or satisfied, God pleased with what is in the heart, the person judging something good

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 8:18 But the Lord said to David my father , ‘ Whereas it was in your heart to build a house for my name , you did well that it was in your heart .
Psa 119:71 It is good for me that I was afflicted , that I might learn your statutes .

groups.context_description: Term names goodness as an inner-being disposition and Spirit-produced fruit — a quality that fills the person and is completed by God, expressed in righteous conduct and resolve

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,

groups.context_description: Term names kindness as God's inner disposition of generous goodwill toward humanity — the divine attribute that leads to repentance, governs judgment and mercy, and is displayed supremely in Christ

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 11:22 Note then the kindness and the severity of God : severity toward those who have fallen , but God’s kindness to you , provided you continue in his kindness . Otherwise you too will be cut off .

groups.context_description: Term names kindness as a Spirit-produced inner quality of the believer — the disposition to act with goodness toward others, listed as fruit of the Spirit and garment of the renewed person

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gal 5:22 But the fruit of the Spirit is love , joy , peace , patience , kindness , goodness , faithfulness ,

groups.context_description: Term names the righteous person's inner moral character — the constitutive orientation of the whole inner being toward what is right before God and others

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 53:11 Out of the anguish of his soul he shall see and be satisfied ; by his knowledge shall the righteous one , my servant , make many to be accounted righteous , and he shall bear their iniquities .
Hab 2:4 “ Behold , his soul is puffed up ; it is not upright within him, but the righteous shall live by his faith .

groups.context_description: Term names righteousness as the standard by which God tests and vindicates — the inner moral condition God sees, approves, rewards, and which may be abandoned

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 7:9 Oh, let the evil of the wicked come to an end , and may you establish the righteous — you who test the minds and hearts , O righteous God !

groups.context_description: Term names the inner response of the righteous person toward God — orientation of trust, joy, and praise

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 64:10 Let the righteous one rejoice in the Lord and take refuge in him! Let all the upright in heart exult !

groups.context_description: Term names righteousness as the inner moral character of the person — right relationship with God and others expressed in just conduct, faithful dealing, and integrity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 21:3 To do righteousness and justice is more acceptable to the Lord than sacrifice .

groups.context_description: Term names righteousness as the status credited to or vindicated in the person by God — moral standing before God, including imputation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 15:6 And he believed the Lord , and he counted it to him as righteousness .

groups.context_description: Term names righteousness as the divine character quality received, longed for, and responded to — gift, stream of salvation, ground of inner hope and exultation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 61:10 I will greatly rejoice in the Lord ; my soul shall exult in my God , for he has clothed me with the garments of salvation ; he has covered me with the robe of righteousness , as a bridegroom decks himself like a priest with a beautiful headdress , and as a bride adorns herself with her jewels .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the person as morally innocent or free from guilt — the inner moral standing of a person before God and the human community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 23:7 Keep far from a false charge , and do not kill the innocent and righteous , for I will not acquit the wicked .
2Sa 3:28 Afterward , when David heard of it, he said , “ I and my kingdom are forever guiltless before the Lord for the blood of Abner the son of Ner .

groups.context_description: Term identifies "innocent blood" as a moral category — the shedding of which constitutes moral pollution and the protection of which is a moral obligation before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 27:25 “‘ Cursed be anyone who takes a bribe to shed innocent blood .’ And all the people shall say , ‘ Amen .’
Jer 22:17 But you have eyes and heart only for your dishonest gain , for shedding innocent blood , and for practicing oppression and violence .”

groups.context_description: Term marks freedom from moral obligation or liability — clearance from a sworn commitment, legal claim, or culpability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 24:41 Then you will be free from my oath , when you come to my clan . And if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath .’

Jos 2:19 Then if anyone goes out of the doors of your house into the street , his blood shall be on his own head , and we shall be guiltless . But if a hand is laid on anyone who is with you in the house , his blood shall be on our head .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the person as morally innocent or free from guilt — the inner moral standing of a person before God and the human community

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 23:7 Keep far from a false charge , and do not kill the innocent and righteous , for I will not acquit the wicked .
2Sa 3:28 Afterward , when David heard of it, he said , “ I and my kingdom are forever guiltless before the Lord for the blood of Abner the son of Ner .

groups.context_description: Term identifies "innocent blood" as a moral category — the shedding of which constitutes moral pollution and the protection of which is a moral obligation before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 27:25 “‘ Cursed be anyone who takes a bribe to shed innocent blood .’ And all the people shall say , ‘ Amen .’
Jer 22:17 But you have eyes and heart only for your dishonest gain , for shedding innocent blood , and for practicing oppression and violence .”

groups.context_description: Term marks freedom from moral obligation or liability — clearance from a sworn commitment, legal claim, or culpability

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 24:41 Then you will be free from my oath , when you come to my clan . And if they will not give her to you, you will be free from my oath .’

Jos 2:19 Then if anyone goes out of the doors of your house into the street , his blood shall be on his own head , and we shall be guiltless . But if a hand is laid on anyone who is with you in the house , his blood shall be on our head .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being quality of innocence — the condition of the heart and hands being free from moral guilt before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:5 Did he not himself say to me, ‘ She is my sister ’? And she herself said , ‘He is my brother .’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this .”
Psa 73:13 All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence .

groups.context_description: Term names the inner-being quality of innocence — the condition of the heart and hands being free from moral guilt before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:5 Did he not himself say to me, ‘ She is my sister ’? And she herself said , ‘He is my brother .’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this .”
Psa 73:13 All in vain have I kept my heart clean and washed my hands in innocence .

groups.context_description: Term names a metaphorical covering of moral reproach — the public declaration of innocence and vindication

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:16 To Sarah he said , “Behold, I have given your brother a thousand pieces of silver . It is a sign of your innocence in the eyes of all who are with you , and before everyone you are vindicated .”

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of covering as the condition of inner vulnerability and exposure — of the poor, and of the realm of death before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 26:6 Sheol is naked before God, and Abaddon has no covering .

groups.context_description: Term frames covering as divine response to vulnerability and divine expression of grief — God's compassion for the exposed and his inner mourning made visible

anchor_verses.verse_text: Exo 22:27 for that is his only covering , and it is his cloak for his body ; in what else shall he sleep ? And if he cries to me, I will hear , for I am compassionate .

groups.context_description: Term names what covers the person in death and judgment — the inverted image of pride and pomp brought to humiliation before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 14:11 Your pomp is brought down to Sheol , the sound of your harps ; maggots are laid as a bed beneath you, and worms are your covers .

groups.context_description: Term frames the self-presentation of a person before God or another — offering the self in availability, surrender, or accountability

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 12:3 Here I am; testify against me before the Lord and before his anointed . Whose ox have I taken ? Or whose donkey have I taken ? Or whom have I defrauded ? Whom have I oppressed ? Or from whose hand have I taken a bribe to blind my eyes with it? Testify against me and I will restore it to you .”
Isa 6:8 And I heard the voice of the Lord saying , “ Whom shall I send , and who will go for us?” Then I said , “ Here I am! Send me .”

groups.context_description: Term frames a first-person or communal confessional disclosure of inner condition — guilt, fear, inadequacy, need, or grief — before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ezr 9:15 O Lord , the God of Israel , you are just , for we are left a remnant that has escaped , as it is today . Behold , we are before you in our guilt , for none can stand before you because of this .”
Psa 51:5 Behold , I was brought forth in iniquity , and in sin did my mother conceive me.

groups.context_description: Term frames a divine disclosure of God's inner orientation — election, delight, care, and relational approach toward his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 42:1 Behold my servant , whom I uphold , my chosen , in whom my soul delights ; I have put my Spirit upon him; he will bring forth justice to the nations .
Isa 49:16 Behold , I have engraved you on the palms of my hands; your walls are continually before me.

groups.context_description: Term frames a moral verdict or exposure of the inner being — what God sees, seeks, or judges in the heart, will, and conduct of persons

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:6 Behold , you delight in truth in the inward being , and you teach me wisdom in the secret heart .

Isa 58:3 ‘ Why have we fasted , and you see it not ? Why have we humbled ourselves , and you take no knowledge of it?’ Behold , in the day of your fast you seek your own pleasure , and oppress all your workers .

groups.context_description: Term frames a personal disclosure of the inner experience of God — searching, hiddenness, helplessness, or submission before divine power

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 9:11 Behold, he passes by me, and I see him not; he moves on , but I do not perceive him .
Job 40:4 “ Behold , I am of small account ; what shall I answer you? I lay my hand on my mouth .

groups.context_description: Term frames a disclosure of inner knowing, wisdom, awe, or affective orientation — including fear of the Lord, understanding, love, grief, and longing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 28:28 And he said to man , ‘ Behold , the fear of the Lord , that is wisdom , and to turn away from evil is understanding .’”
Song 1:15 Behold , you are beautiful , my love ; behold , you are beautiful ; your eyes are doves .

groups.context_description: Term marks the temporal extent or ongoing duration of an inner-being state, divine relationship, or orientation — establishing how long the inner condition has persisted or been sustained

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 1:16 Do not regard your servant as a worthless woman , for all along I have been speaking out of my great anxiety and vexation .”
Psa 71:17 O God , from my youth you have taught me, and I still proclaim your wondrous deeds .

groups.context_description: Term frames the self-presentation of a person before God in obedience, availability, or acknowledgment of inadequacy

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 22:1 After these things God tested Abraham and said to him, “ Abraham !” And he said , “ Here I am.”
Jer 1:6 Then I said , “ Ah , Lord God ! Behold , I do not know how to speak , for I am only a youth .”

groups.context_description: Term frames a first-person or communal disclosure of inner need, grief, fear, or moral condition before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 15:3 And Abram said , “ Behold , you have given me no offspring , and a member of my household will be my heir .”
Neh 9:36 Behold , we are slaves this day ; in the land that you gave to our fathers to enjoy its fruit and its good gifts , behold , we are slaves .

groups.context_description: Term introduces a theophanic vision or divine encounter — making the divine presence directly present to perception

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 28:12 And he dreamed , and behold , there was a ladder set up on the earth , and the top of it reached to heaven . And behold , the angels of God were ascending and descending on it !
Gen 28:13 And behold , the Lord stood above it and said , “ I am the Lord , the God of Abraham your father and the God of Isaac . The land on which you lie I will give to you and to your offspring .

groups.context_description: Term introduces a divine disclosure of God's orientation of love, promise, care, or attentiveness toward his people

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 16:11 And the angel of the Lord said to her, “ Behold , you are pregnant and shall bear a son . You shall call his name Ishmael , because the Lord has listened to your affliction .
Hos 2:14 “ Therefore , behold , I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness , and speak tenderly to her.

groups.context_description: Term introduces a moral verdict, exposure of inner sin or deception, or inner transformation through divine action

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 6:7 And he touched my mouth and said : “ Behold , this has touched your lips ; your guilt is taken away , and your sin atoned for .”
Hab 2:4 “ Behold , his soul is puffed up ; it is not upright within him, but the righteous shall live by his faith .

groups.context_description: Term introduces inner wisdom reflection, longing, moral restraint, or the recognition of inner truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: A Song of Ascents . Of David . Psa 133:1 Behold, how good and pleasant it is when brothers dwell in unity !

Ecc 2:11 Then I considered all that my hands had done and the toil I had expended in doing it , and behold , all was vanity and a striving after wind , and there was nothing to be gained under the sun .

groups.context_description: Term introduces the inner experience of anguish, dread, despair, hope, or emotional intensity before God or in life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 3:7 Behold , let that night be barren ; let no joyful cry enter it .
Job 32:19 Behold , my belly is like wine that has no vent ; like new wineskins ready to burst .

groups.context_description: Term names the place associated with the moral abomination of child sacrifice — an act explicitly contrary to the divine inner will and a marker of the depths of human spiritual corruption

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 33:6 And he burned his sons as an offering in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom , and used fortune-telling and omens and sorcery , and dealt with mediums and with necromancers . He did much evil in the sight of the Lord , provoking him to anger .

Jer 7:31 And they have built the high places of Topheth , which is in the Valley of the Son of Hinnom , to burn their sons and their daughters in the fire , which I did not command , nor did it come into my mind .

groups.context_description: Term names the absence of soundness as the condition of the person or people under divine discipline or moral corruption — linking physical and inner brokenness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 38:3 There is no soundness in my flesh because of your indignation ; there is no health in my bones because of my sin .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the person as inwardly blameless and morally whole — the quality of inner character that defines one who fears God and turns from evil

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job , and that man was blameless and upright , one who feared God and turned away from evil .
Psa 37:37 Mark the blameless and behold the upright , for there is a future for the man of peace .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the beloved as wholly complete and perfect — the wholeness that evokes delight and singular devotion

anchor_verses.verse_text: Song 5:2 I slept , but my heart was awake . A sound ! My beloved is knocking . “ Open to me, my sister , my love , my dove , my perfect one , for my head is wet with dew , my locks with the drops of the night .”

Song 6:9 My dove , my perfect one , is the only one , the only one of her mother , pure to her who bore her. The young women saw her and called her blessed ; the queens and concubines also, and they praised her .

groups.context_description: Term names integrity as the inner moral condition of the person — the wholeness and blamelessness of heart and conduct that characterizes the one who walks rightly before God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 20:5 Did he not himself say to me, ‘ She is my sister ’? And she herself said , ‘He is my brother .’ In the integrity of my heart and the innocence of my hands I have done this .”
Of David . Psa 26:1 Vindicate me, O Lord , for I have walked in my integrity , and I have trusted in the Lord without wavering .

groups.context_description: Term characterizes the person as inwardly blameless and whole before God — the quality of heart and conduct that constitutes right standing and walking in God's presence

anchor_verses.verse_text: Gen 17:1 When Abram was ninety-nine years old the Lord appeared to Abram and said to him, “ I am God Almighty ; walk before me, and be blameless ,
Psa 15:2 He who walks blamelessly and does what is right and speaks truth in his heart ;

groups.context_description: Term names the Thummim as the instrument of divine disclosure of inner moral guilt — the means by which God reveals what is hidden in the person or community

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Sa 14:41 Therefore Saul said , “O Lord God of Israel , why have you not answered your servant this day ? If this guilt is in me or in Jonathan my son , O Lord , God of Israel , give Urim . But if this guilt is in your people Israel , give Thummim .” And Jonathan and Saul were taken , but the people escaped .

groups.context_description: Term names integrity as the inner quality that the person holds fast and presents before God — the moral wholeness that defines character and guides conduct under pressure

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 2:3 And the Lord said to Satan , “Have you considered my servant Job , that there is none like him on the earth , a blameless and upright man , who fears God and turns away from evil ? He still holds fast his integrity , although you incited me against him to destroy him without reason .”
Pro 11:3 The integrity of the upright guides them, but the crookedness of the treacherous destroys them .

groups.context_description: Term characterises God's way, works, and knowledge as perfect — the inner perfection of divine character and action as the ground of trust

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 32:4 “The Rock , his work is perfect , for all his ways are justice . A God of faithfulness and without iniquity , just and upright is he .
Job 37:16 Do you know the balancings of the clouds , the wondrous works of him who is perfect in knowledge ,

groups.context_description: Term characterises the Torah as perfect — its completeness as the source of inner renewal of the soul

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 19:7 The law of the Lord is perfect , reviving the soul ; the testimony of the Lord is sure , making wise the simple ;

groups.context_description: Term names the eschatological transformation of the person — the putting on of incorruptibility and immortality at resurrection

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Cor 15:42 So is it with the resurrection of the dead . What is sown is perishable ; what is raised is imperishable .
1Cor 15:53 For this perishable body must put on the imperishable , and this mortal body must put on immortality .

groups.context_description: Term names incorruptibility as the quality of inner love and the inner orientation of those who seek God's glory

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:7 to those who by patience in well-doing seek for glory and honor and immortality , he will give eternal life ;
Eph 6:24 Grace be with all who love our Lord Jesus Christ with love incorruptible .

groups.context_description: Term names moral and relational purity as an inner character disposition — the quality of heart and motivation governing conduct and relationships

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 4:12 Let no one despise you for your youth , but set the believers an example in speech , in conduct , in love , in faith , in purity .

groups.context_description: Term names the act of inner moral and spiritual purification — soul, heart, and self purified through obedience, hope, and turning toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Jo 3:3 And everyone who thus hopes in him purifies himself as he is pure .

groups.context_description: Term names ritual purification as an act of preparation expressing inner orientation of readiness and covenantal reverence toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 21:26 Then Paul took the men , and the next day he purified himself along with them and went into the temple , giving notice when the days of purification would be fulfilled and the offering presented for each one of them .

groups.context_description: Term names the structured purification process as an act of ritual preparation expressing inner readiness and covenantal participation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Act 21:26 Then Paul took the men , and the next day he purified himself along with them and went into the temple , giving notice when the days of purification would be fulfilled and the offering presented for each one of them .

groups.context_description: Term describes the morally pure inner character — the quality of heart, mind, and motivation that is free from defilement and moral corruption

anchor_verses.verse_text: Jam 3:17 But the wisdom from above is first pure , then peaceable , gentle , open to reason , full of mercy and good fruits , impartial and sincere .

groups.context_description: Term describes relational and covenantal purity — inner disposition of fidelity and undivided orientation in relationship to Christ or within human relationships

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 11:2 For I feel a divine jealousy for you , since I betrothed you to one husband , to present you as a pure virgin to Christ .

groups.context_description: Term names purity as an inner character quality among the dispositions through which authentic apostolic ministry is expressed

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 6:6 by purity , knowledge , patience , kindness , the Holy Spirit , genuine love ;

groups.context_description: Term qualifies inner motivation — names pure and sincere motivation as the inner disposition from which authentic action ought to flow, in contrast to selfish ambition

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 1:17 The former proclaim Christ out of selfish ambition , not sincerely but thinking to afflict me in my imprisonment .

groups.context_description: Term describes the purity of the heart and inner person as moral and spiritual integrity before God — an inner life free from corruption and oriented toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:10 Create in me a clean heart , O God , and renew a right spirit within me.

groups.context_description: Term describes purity attributed to God's word and the fear of the Lord — the quality of inner spiritual integrity that characterises divine revelation and right orientation toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 19:9 the fear of the Lord is clean , enduring forever ; the rules of the Lord are true , and righteous altogether .

groups.context_description: Term describes the covenantal and spiritual restoration of the inner person — being made clean before God through divine action, removing inner defilement and renewing covenantal relationship

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 36:25 I will sprinkle clean water on you, and you shall be clean from all your uncleannesses , and from all your idols I will cleanse you .

groups.context_description: Term describes ritual/levitical cleanness as a covenantal condition governing access to God and sacred space — the state of being clean as prerequisite for worship and community life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 10:10 You are to distinguish between the holy and the common , and between the unclean and the clean ,

groups.context_description: Term describes the absolute moral purity of God's character — an inner quality of divine nature that is incompatible with evil or wrongdoing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Hab 1:13 You who are of purer eyes than to see evil and cannot look at wrong , why do you idly look at traitors and remain silent when the wicked swallows up the man more righteous than he ?

groups.context_description: Term describes the purification of the inner person from sin and moral defilement — the heart and soul made pure through divine cleansing, repentance, and spiritual renewal

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 51:2 Wash me thoroughly from my iniquity , and cleanse me from my sin !

groups.context_description: Term describes ritual cleansing as a covenantal act of transition — the person made ritually clean through prescribed acts, enabling restored participation in sacred community

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ki 5:10 And Elisha sent a messenger to him, saying , “ Go and wash in the Jordan seven times , and your flesh shall be restored , and you shall be clean .”

groups.context_description: Term describes God's purifying action upon priests, Levites, and people — cleansing for restored covenantal service and communal life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Mal 3:3 He will sit as a refiner and purifier of silver , and he will purify the sons of Levi and refine them like gold and silver , and they will bring offerings in righteousness to the Lord .

groups.context_description: Term names the ritual period of purification following childbirth — the covenantal state of the post-partum woman and her transition back to full community participation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 12:4 Then she shall continue for thirty-three days in the blood of her purifying . She shall not touch anything holy , nor come into the sanctuary , until the days of her purifying are completed .

groups.context_description: Term names the ritual purification period as a state of cleansing — the post-partum woman's covenantal condition during and after the prescribed period of purification

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 12:4 Then she shall continue for thirty-three days in the blood of her purifying . She shall not touch anything holy , nor come into the sanctuary , until the days of her purifying are completed .

groups.context_description: Term names the prescribed purification procedure as the covenantal pathway governing a person's return to community and sacred participation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lev 14:2 “This shall be the law of the leprous person for the day of his cleansing . He shall be brought to the priest ,

groups.context_description: Term names purification as a covenantal service and inner-oriented commitment — the person setting their heart toward God even where external cleanness requirements are imperfectly met

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Ch 30:19 who sets his heart to seek God , the Lord , the God of his fathers , even though not according to the sanctuary’s rules of cleanness .”

groups.context_description: Term names the dikaiōma — whether decree, standard, righteous act, or verdict — as the external reality that discloses, qualifies, or re-orients inner-being standing, moral orientation, and response (conformity, defiance, reception of verdict, or worship of divine righteousness revealed).

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 5:16 And the free gift is not like the result of that one man’s sin . For the judgment following one trespass brought condemnation , but the free gift following many trespasses brought justification .
Rev 15:4 Who will not fear , O Lord , and glorify your name ? For you alone are holy . All nations will come and worship you , for your righteous acts have been revealed .”

groups.context_description: Term names sincerity as an inner quality of unmixed, genuine motivation — the condition of acting from pure inner integrity before God rather than from calculation or performance

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Cor 1:12 For our boast is this , the testimony of our conscience , that we behaved in the world with simplicity and godly sincerity , not by earthly wisdom but by the grace of God , and supremely so toward you .

groups.context_description: Term names inner purity or sincerity — the quality of being unmixed, genuine, and morally uncontaminated in inner disposition

anchor_verses.verse_text: 2Pe 3:1 This is now the second letter that I am writing to you , beloved . In both of them I am stirring up your sincere mind by way of reminder ,

groups.context_description: Truth as transforming/shaping force on the inner person — sanctification, purification, self-orientation

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 17:17 Sanctify them in the truth ; your word is truth .

groups.context_description: Truth as the proper object of the will — accepting, obeying, refusing, or rejecting truth as inner act

anchor_verses.verse_text: Rom 2:8 but for those who are self-seeking and do not obey the truth , but obey unrighteousness , there will be wrath and fury .

groups.context_description: Truth as inner quality of character — integrity, authenticity, living and speaking truthfully

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Jo 1:6 If we say we have fellowship with him while we walk in darkness , we lie and do not practice the truth .

groups.context_description: Truth as the Spirit's identity and work in the inner person — Spirit of truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 14:17 even the Spirit of truth , whom the world cannot receive , because it neither sees him nor knows him . You know him , for he dwells with you and will be in you .

groups.context_description: Truth as relational/theological identity — who Christ is; worship in spirit and truth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 4:23 But the hour is coming , and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth , for the Father is seeking such people to worship him .

groups.context_description: Truth as the gospel received inwardly — knowledge of truth as saving and formational event

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ti 2:4 who desires all people to be saved and to come to the knowledge of the truth .

groups.context_description: Truthful speech as inner act of integrity — in relationship and spiritual growth

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eph 4:15 Rather , speaking the truth in love , we are to grow up in every way into him who is the head , into Christ ,

groups.context_description: Truthfulness as inner character quality — integrity, genuineness, the true heart

anchor_verses.verse_text: Phili 4:8 Finally , brothers , whatever is true , whatever is honorable , whatever is just , whatever is pure , whatever is lovely , whatever is commendable , if there is any excellence , if there is anything worthy of praise , think about these things .

groups.context_description: Genuineness as inner-being orientation — true worship, turning to the true God, inner fidelity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 10:22 let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith , with our hearts sprinkled clean from an evil conscience and our bodies washed with pure water .

groups.context_description: "Truly" as qualifier of inner-being recognition, knowing, confession, and reception

anchor_verses.verse_text: Joh 1:47 Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said of him , “ Behold , an Israelite indeed , in whom there is no deceit !”

groups.context_description: Uprightness as an inner quality of the heart — tested by God, contrasted with wickedness, invoked as protection

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ch 29:17 I know , my God , that you test the heart and have pleasure in uprightness . In the uprightness of my heart I have freely offered all these things , and now I have seen your people , who are present here, offering freely and joyously to you .

groups.context_description: Uprightness as the path/way — inner orientation expressed in the direction of life

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 14:2 Whoever walks in uprightness fears the Lord , but he who is devious in his ways despises him .

groups.context_description: Uprightness expressed in speech — words as disclosure of inner integrity

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 33:3 My words declare the uprightness of my heart , and what my lips know they speak sincerely .

groups.context_description: Regnal moral assessment — "did right/what was right in the eyes of the Lord" — moral orientation of the will under divine evaluation

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 15:5 because David did what was right in the eyes of the Lord and did not turn aside from anything that he commanded him all the days of his life , except in the matter of Uriah the Hittite .

groups.context_description: "Right in his own eyes" — moral self-determination; inner conscience as its own arbiter

anchor_verses.verse_text: Judg 17:6 In those days there was no king in Israel . Everyone did what was right in his own eyes .

groups.context_description: "The upright" / "upright in heart" — uprightness as stable inner moral character, especially in Psalms and Proverbs

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 7:10 My shield is with God , who saves the upright in heart .

groups.context_description: Uprightness as divine character — God and his word described as upright/right

anchor_verses.verse_text: Deu 32:4 “The Rock , his work is perfect , for all his ways are justice . A God of faithfulness and without iniquity , just and upright is he .

groups.context_description: Job as the upright man — blameless and upright as defining inner character under testing

anchor_verses.verse_text: Job 1:1 There was a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job , and that man was blameless and upright , one who feared God and turned away from evil .

groups.context_description: "God made man upright, but they have sought out many schemes" — original moral constitution and its departure

anchor_verses.verse_text: Ecc 7:29 See , this alone I found , that God made man upright , but they have sought out many schemes .

groups.context_description: Uprightness of heart directed toward God — inner moral orientation of relational fidelity

anchor_verses.verse_text: 1Ki 3:6 And Solomon said , “ You have shown great and steadfast love to your servant David my father , because he walked before you in faithfulness , in righteousness , and in uprightness of heart toward you. And you have kept for him this great and steadfast love and have given him a son to sit on his throne this day .

groups.context_description: Uprightness as the standard and quality of right judgment — divine and demanded of human authorities

anchor_verses.verse_text: Psa 9:8 and he judges the world with righteousness ; he judges the peoples with uprightness .

groups.context_description: Uprightness as inner character formation — wisdom's goal; the inmost being rejoices at rightness

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 23:16 My inmost being will exult when your lips speak what is right .

groups.context_description: "Before the face/presence of the Lord" — relational orientation of prayer, lament, and honest speech toward God

anchor_verses.verse_text: Lam 2:19 “ Arise , cry out in the night , at the beginning of the night watches ! Pour out your heart like water before the presence of the Lord ! Lift your hands to him for the lives of your children , who faint for hunger at the head of every street .”

groups.context_description: "Before their faces" — the stumbling block of iniquity as the primary object of inner attention (Ezekiel)

anchor_verses.verse_text: Eze 14:3 “ Son of man , these men have taken their idols into their hearts , and set the stumbling block of their iniquity before their faces . Should I indeed let myself be consulted by them ?

groups.context_description: "Directly forward" — inner directedness; focused and undistracted attention

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 4:25 Let your eyes look directly forward , and your gaze be straight before you .

groups.context_description: Straightforwardness as honest inner character — rightness of speech, claims, and conduct

anchor_verses.verse_text: Pro 24:26 Whoever gives an honest answer kisses the lips .

groups.context_description: Uprightness resisted, absent, or refused — the inner being's failure to receive or produce what is right

anchor_verses.verse_text: Isa 30:10 who say to the seers , “Do not see ,” and to the prophets , “Do not prophesy to us what is right ; speak to us smooth things , prophesy illusions ,

groups.context_description: Uprightness as the defining moral character of Christ's kingship — inner quality expressed in sovereign rule

anchor_verses.verse_text: Heb 1:8 But of the Son he says, “ Your throne , O God , is forever and ever , the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom .
